behavior_summary: U8P_GREENTRE::look is the 'U8P_GREENTRE::look' (examine) handler for the green tree object. When a player (or relevant actor) inspects the tree it records/marks item information, adjusts per-player exclusion/processing state, and then requests the item to 'bark' a short description string 'tree ' to the viewer.

Sequence: set object info (info id 0x0208) on the tree instance, run a process/exclude adjustment involving the viewer pid, prepare the literal string 'tree ' and call Item::bark on the tree to present the text to the viewer, free the temporary string, evaluate the bark return value in an implication/pop pattern into a small temp, then suspend (yield) before returning.
notable_control_flow: Entry is event-driven: Event 0 (U8P_GREENTRE::look) triggers U8P_GREENTRE::look; the handler performs a linear sequence of state changes and a display call then suspends.

The return value from Item::bark is inspected (used in an 'implies' style check) and stored into a temporary before the routine suspends — this implies the bark return may influence immediate post-bark control/logic (exact effect is not present in the snippet).
No global flags are modified by this script; behavior is local to the item instance and the viewer's context.
open_questions: What concrete semantic meaning does info id 0x0208 represent in the game's info table (e.g., 'examined' flag, encyclopedia entry, or tooltip population)?
What are the two indirect bytes pushed to 'set info' (their meaning and how they affect displayed info) — they are present in the call but not named in the disassembly summary.
What exact effect does the 'process exclude' call have for this object-player pair (temporary visibility/exclusion, interaction lockout, or UI filtering)?
What does the Item::bark return value encode (success/failure, user dismissed vs. ignored) that requires an 'implies' check before suspend?

main_function.name: U8P_GREENTRE::look
main_function.purpose: Handle a player/actor 'U8P_GREENTRE::look' event for the green tree object by marking item info/state for the viewer, adjusting processing/exclusion state for that viewer, and presenting a short descriptive text ('tree ') via the item's bark routine, then suspending.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_GREENTRE::look) — triggered when a player/actor looks at or examines the green tree object.

function_map.name: U8P_GREENTRE::look
function_map.role: Event handler for the object's 'U8P_GREENTRE::look' interaction; manages per-viewer info/exclusion and emits the object's short description.

external_calls.purpose: Record or populate object information for the target item using info id 0x0208 and two bytes of indirect data — likely marks the item as examined or stores view-specific info.

Adjust processing/exclusion state of the object with respect to the viewer PID (likely to prevent duplicate interactions, control visibility, or otherwise modulate further processing while the bark/display occurs).
Request the item instance to display/emit a short text message ('tree ') to the specified viewer/pid. This is the user-facing description shown when the tree is examined.
Free the temporary string buffer allocated for the bark message after Item::bark returns.
Yield the process after performing the bark and post-call bookkeeping — typical for scripted item interactions that pause until UI/dialog is dismissed or next event.
